description: The scene begins outside a brick apartment building on a snowy day as a yellow taxi drives past. The setting changes to an apartment interior where Monica and Rachel sit at a round table decorating cookies. Ross sits on a sofa in the background, holding an object. By the window, Phoebe turns toward the room.
Phoebe looks back out the window and says, "Ugly Naked Guy is decorating his tree," her voice high-pitched and strained.
Phoebe continues to look out the window.
Phoebe turns her head back toward the room and says, "And, oh, my God, you should see the size of his Christmas balls."
On the sofa, Ross holds a silver menorah and a small tool, looking up with his mouth slightly open. The door opens, and Joey and Chandler enter the apartment.
Joey and Chandler walk toward the kitchen area. Joey asks, "Hey, how much did you guys tip the super this year?"
Chandler, holding a pen and a small notepad, says, "Yeah, we were going to give 50, but if you guys gave more, we don't want to look bad."
Monica, squeezing icing onto a cookie from a bag, says, "Oh, actually, this year we just made him homemade cookies."
Chandler looks down at his notepad.
Chandler writes on his notepad and says, "And 25 it is."
Monica stops squeezing icing and looks toward Chandler.
Joey tilts his head and asks, "You gave him cookies?"
Phoebe walks over from the window, holding a cookie. She says, "Money is so impersonal. Cookie says someone really cares," and takes a bite.
Phoebe chews the cookie while holding it up.
Monica holds her icing bag with both hands and says, "All right, we're broke. But cookies do say that."
Phoebe takes another bite of her cookie.
Phoebe, chewing her bite, says, "I can see that. A plate of brownies once told me a limerick."
Chandler and Joey stand near the kitchen counter. Phoebe walks past them toward the sink, holding a glass. Chandler turns his head to watch her.
Chandler points a finger at Phoebe and asks, "Pheebs. Let me ask you something. Were these funny brownies?"
Phoebe stops walking and looks back at Chandler.
Phoebe, holding her glass and cookie, shakes her head and says, "Not especially."
Phoebe takes another bite of her cookie.
Phoebe chews and says, "But you know what? I think they had pot in them."
Chandler presses his lips together and nods slightly. Joey stands silently beside him.
Ross walks into the kitchen area and asks, "So, you guys, who else did you tip with cookies?"
Monica points her icing bag toward Ross.
Monica gestures with her icing bag and says, "The mailman, the super," while Rachel adds, "Oh, and the newspaper delivery guy."
Joey opens the apartment door and looks down.
Joey looks at the floor and says softly, "Oh, my God."
Rachel asks, "What?"
Joey bends down toward the floor in the hallway.
Joey stands back up with an item in his hands and says, "I don't think you're going to like this."
Joey unrolls a crumpled newspaper.
Joey holds up the torn newspaper. Monica says, "Oh, gosh." Rachel touches the paper and says, "Oh, there's a cookie smashed in the sports section."
Ross takes the torn newspaper from Joey.
Monica points at the torn paper in Ross's hands and says, "Look. And he did my crossword puzzle."
Ross looks at the paper and says, "Yeah, but not very well. Unless 14 across, Gershwin musical actually is"
Ross reads from the paper, "bite me, bite me, bite me, bite"
Ross finishes reading the paper, and the scene darkens to black as instrumental transition music begins to play.

The setting changes to the exterior of a coffee shop named "Central Perk," decorated with lights and fake snow, while the music fades. The view shifts inside to a lounge area where Joey sits in an armchair while Chandler and Monica sit on a sofa.
Joey, holding a mug, says, "I can't believe it's Christmas already."
Joey gestures with his free hand and continues, "You know, I mean, one day you're eating turkey, the next thing you know, your lords are leaping"
Joey finishes his sentence, "and your geese are laying."
Chandler looks up from the newspaper in his lap.
Chandler says, "Which is why geese are so relaxed this time of year."
Ross walks into the coffee shop carrying two large paper bags.
Ross walks toward the counter and says, "Hey, guys."
Monica, Chandler, and Joey respond simultaneously, "Hey," as Ross approaches Rachel standing behind the counter.
Ross stops across the counter from Rachel and says, "Hey, Rach."
Ross sets one of his bags down on the counter.
Ross looks at Rachel and says, "I got you a little present."
Rachel looks at Ross with a neutral expression. Ross reaches his hands into his pockets.
Ross pulls a small metallic object from his pocket and says, "I'll open it."
Ross manipulates the small metal Slinky toy in his hands.
Ross smiles, holds the Slinky out toward Rachel, and says, "Just a Slinky."
Ross begins passing the Slinky back and forth between his hands.
Ross bounces the Slinky between his hands and says, "Remember for walks downstairs, a loner in pairs?"
Ross continues, "Everyone knows this"
Ross continues to handle the Slinky, bringing his hands closer together.
Ross stops bouncing the Slinky and says, "just a big spring."
Ross keeps his gaze on Rachel, holding the toy still.
Ross gestures with one hand and says, "All right, you're still mad at me because of the whole horrible and degrading list"
Ross finishes, "of reasons not to be with me? How about from now on, we"
Ross adds, "just call it the unfortunate incident?"
Rachel stares at Ross without changing her expression. Ross shifts his gaze away and spots Gunther.
Ross holds out the Slinky and says, "Hey, Gunther, you got stairs in your place?" while Gunther nods and replies, "Yeah, yeah."
Ross hands the Slinky to Gunther and says, "Go nuts. There we go."
Ross picks up his paper bags and walks over to the sofa area.
Ross approaches the group and says, "Hey, guys."
Monica, Chandler, and Joey respond, "Hey."
Ross sits on the edge of the coffee table.
Joey points to the bags and asks, "What's in the bag?" Ross replies, "Just some presents." Joey says, "Come on, show us what you bought."
Joey smiles and leans his head back slightly in his armchair.
Joey says, "You know you want to."
Ross looks down at the floor, then nods slightly.
Ross looks up and says, "Okay."
Ross sets the bags on his lap and reaches inside one of them.
Ross pulls out a framed photograph, holds it up for the others to see, and says, "Okay. This is a picture frame from Ben to my parents."
Monica leans forward and smiles at the picture frame.
Monica smiles as Ross pulls out two folded towels with "HERS" embroidered on them, saying, "I got some hers and hers towels for Susan and Carol."
Joey gives a double thumbs-up and nods.
Inside a coffee shop, Monica and Chandler sit on a sofa. Ross sits beside them, holding up a white jacket on a hanger, while Rachel stands nearby.
"And I got this blouse for Mom," Ross says, gesturing toward the jacket.
"Ross, that is gorgeous. Look at these. Authentic fake medals," Monica says, pointing at the jacket.
"Tell you, Mom's going to be voted best dressed at the make-believe military," Monica continues, while Ross examines the jacket.
"academy," Monica finishes.
Ross begins folding the jacket. Phoebe walks into the coffee shop wearing a brown coat and carrying a bag over her shoulder.
"Hey," Phoebe says.
Ross and Rachel greet her in return as Phoebe approaches the sofa.
"Happy Christmas Eve Eve," Rachel says. "You too," Phoebe replies. Looking down at the picture frame on Ross's lap, Phoebe says, "Oh, my God."
"Where did you get this?" Phoebe asks. "Macy's, third floor, home furnishings," Ross answers.
Phoebe picks up the framed photograph from Ross's lap.
"This is my father," Phoebe says, looking at the photo.
"This is a picture of my dad," Phoebe repeats.
Joey, sitting in an armchair nearby, furrows his brow. Chandler looks at Phoebe with his mouth slightly open.
"Now, Phoebe, that's the guy that comes in the frame," Chandler says.
"What? This is my dad. All right, I'll show you," Phoebe says, taking off her coat.
"Phoebe, I thought your dad was in prison," Rachel says as Phoebe rummages through her shoulder bag.
"No, no, that's my stepdad. My real dad's the one that ran out on us before I was born," Phoebe says, pulling an item from her bag.
Phoebe continues to look at her hands.
"How have you never been on Oprah?" Chandler asks.
Phoebe opens a small photo wallet.
"Okay, look. See? This is him," Phoebe says, pointing to a small photograph inside the wallet.
"My mother gave me this picture before she died. Same guy," Phoebe says, holding the wallet out toward Rachel.
Ross looks down closely at the picture frame in his lap. Chandler and Monica keep their gaze on Phoebe.
"Honey, this is a picture of the frame guy posing in front of a bright blue screen," Monica says, gesturing with one hand.
"with a collie," Monica finishes.
Phoebe turns her head toward Monica.
"Not a blue screen," Phoebe says.
Phoebe lowers her gaze to the photo wallet.
"Maybe it was just really clear that day," Phoebe says.
Phoebe puts the photo wallet away and picks up her bag.
"Okay, I have to talk to my grandmother," Phoebe says, turning and walking rapidly away from the group.
The others watch her walk away.
"Wait a minute, honey," Monica calls out, with overlapping voices from the others.
Phoebe exits the coffee shop. The others keep their heads turned toward the door.
"Wow," Joey says.
Joey holds a mug, sitting with his right leg crossed over his left knee.
"So anyway, I'm trying to get my boss's ex-wife to sleep with me," Joey says.
Monica, Chandler, and Ross turn their heads sharply to face Joey.
"But when Phoebe has a problem, everyone's all ears," Joey says, extending his left arm outward.
The setting changes to an apartment. Frances sits at a round table, holding an open newspaper and a pencil.
"Esther Livingston," Frances reads aloud, drawing a line through text in the newspaper with her pencil.
Frances continues marking the page.
"gone," Frances says.
Phoebe opens the door and walks into the apartment.
"Hi, Gram," Phoebe says.
Phoebe lowers her bag from her shoulder.
"Hi," Frances says.
"What you doing?" Phoebe asks. "Oh, just updating the phone book," Frances replies, keeping her eyes on the newspaper.
Phoebe sits in a chair across the table from Frances.
"Gram, can I see the pictures of my dad again?" Phoebe asks.
Frances looks up from the newspaper.
"Sure," Frances says.
Frances pushes her chair back and stands up.
"How come?" Frances asks, stepping out of the frame. "Just to see him," Phoebe says.
"Oh, sure," Frances says off-screen.
Frances returns to the table, carrying a decorated box.
"All right. This is the one of your father in a meadow," Frances says, opening the box and handing Phoebe a small framed picture.
Phoebe takes the picture while Frances reaches back into the box.
"and helping a little boy fly a kite," Frances says, handing Phoebe another frame.
Phoebe accepts the second frame.
"And here he is at a graduation," Frances says, passing a third frame.
Phoebe takes the frame.
"Another graduation," Frances says, handing over a fourth frame.
Frances retrieves another frame from the box.
"Another graduation," Frances says, passing the fifth frame.
Phoebe holds one of the frames up, examining it.
"Okay, is this really my father?" Phoebe asks.
Frances's lips part in a smile.
"Well, of course it is," Frances says, laughing briefly while speaking.
Phoebe keeps her gaze on the frame in her hands.
"I smell smoke," Phoebe says.
"Maybe that's because someone's pants are on fire," Phoebe continues.
Frances stops smiling and looks directly at Phoebe.
"In all the years that we have been grandmother and granddaughter, you have never lied to me," Phoebe says.
Frances raises one finger, then rests her hand on the table.
"All right, that is not your father. That's just a picture of a guy in a frame," Frances says.
Phoebe shifts her gaze down to the table.
"Oh, God," Phoebe says, her volume lower than her previous line.
Frances leans forward slightly.
"Your mother's idea," Frances says.
Phoebe keeps her head bowed.
"She didn't want you to know your real father because it hurt her so much when he left," Frances says.
"And I didn't want to go along with it. But," Frances continues.
"well, then she died and it was harder to argue with her," Frances finishes.
Phoebe rests her arms on the table, still looking down.
"Not impossible, but harder," Frances says.
Phoebe raises her head to look at Frances.
"All right, so he's not a famous tree surgeon," Phoebe says.
Frances looks at Phoebe without speaking.
In a living room, Frances and Phoebe sit facing each other. Frances begins speaking.
Frances looks at Phoebe and says, "And then, I guess, okay, he doesn't live in a hut in Burma where there's no phones."
"Last I heard, he was a pharmacist," Frances continues.
"somewhere upstate," Frances adds.
Phoebe crosses her arms and says, "Okay, that makes no sense."
Phoebe leans forward slightly, asking, "Why would the villagers worship a pharmacist?"
Frances looks at Phoebe, her lips pressed together.
"Honey," Frances says, her head tilted slightly.
Phoebe's eyes widen slightly and she lowers her gaze.
"Oh, yeah. Anyway," Phoebe says, looking back up.
Frances raises her hands briefly and says, "That's all I know."
Frances reaches to her side, picks up a small wooden frame, and opens it.
"That," Frances says, removing a small photo from the frame.
She closes the frame and holds the photo out toward Phoebe.
"and this," Frances adds as Phoebe takes the photo.
Phoebe looks down at the photo in her hand. Frances watches her.
Phoebe continues studying the photo in silence.
"is the real him," Frances says.
Phoebe continues looking at the photo.
"Oh," Phoebe says softly.
The setting changes to an apartment where Monica, Rachel, Chandler, and Joey are decorating a Christmas tree. Chandler sits on a stepladder.
Chandler gestures with his hands as he speaks. "You know, I remember my father all dressed up in the red suit," he says.
"the big black boots and the patent leather belt," Chandler adds. Rachel and Joey continue decorating the tree.
"sneaking around downstairs. He didn't want anybody to see him," Chandler continues, moving his hands. Rachel turns to look at him.
"But he'd be drunk. So he'd stumble, crash into something, and wake everybody," Chandler adds.
"up," Chandler finishes. Rachel pushes her hair back and says, "That doesn't sound like a very merry Christmas."
Chandler looks at her and says, "Who said anything about Christmas?"
Rachel stares at Chandler, her mouth slightly open. Joey stops decorating and looks at Chandler. Audience laughter is heard. The apartment door opens.
Monica enters the apartment followed by Ross, carrying grocery bags. "Hi," she says.
"Hey. Anyone hear from Phoebe yet?" Ross asks, closing the door. "No, nothing," Rachel replies, turning toward them.
Monica places her bags on a table and says, "I hope she's okay." Ross says, "Yeah. I know exactly what she's going through."
Monica holds a green plastic container, looking at Ross. "How do you know exactly what she's going through?" she asks. "She told us," Joey replies.
Monica and Ross look toward Joey. Audience laughter is heard.
"So what you got there, Monica?" Chandler asks, standing near the tree.
Monica pulls items from the grocery bags.
"Some stuff for the party," Monica replies. Ross looks toward Chandler and Joey.
"What are you guys doing here? Are you supposed to be Christmas shopping? You guys haven't gotten your" Ross says.
"presents yet? Tomorrow's Christmas Eve. What are you going to do?" Monica asks, looking at Chandler and Joey.
Chandler replies, "Don't you have to be Claymation to say stuff like that?"
Monica stares at Chandler with a flat expression. Audience laughter is heard. Rachel walks over to the table.
"Oh, by the way, Mon, I don't think the mailman liked your cookies. Here are the ornaments your mom sent," Rachel says, holding up a crushed brown paper package.
Monica looks at the crushed package in Rachel's hands. Chandler and Joey stand nearby.
"Well, maybe the mailman liked the cookies. We just didn't give him enough," Monica says, taking the package.
Joey says, "Monica, pigeons learn faster than you."
Monica and Ross look at Joey. Audience laughter is heard.
The group disperses. Rachel walks toward the kitchen counter, and Ross follows her.
"Hey, Rach. You know what?" Ross says, standing behind Rachel as she turns to face him.
"I think I know what'll make you feel better. How about you make a list about me?" Ross says, pointing to himself.
Rachel waves her hands dismissively. "Forget it, Ross. No, I'm not going to"
"stand here and make a list of" Rachel says, then pauses as Ross motions for her to continue.
"okay, you're whiny, you're protective, you are insecure, you're" Rachel continues rapidly, counting on her fingers.
"gutless," Rachel adds.
"You don't just sort of seize the day. You liked me for, what, a year, and you didn't do anything about it," Rachel says, gesturing broadly.
"and, oh, you wear too much of" she continues, stepping closer to him.
"that gel in your hair," Rachel finishes.
Ross stares at her, his jaw tense. Audience laughter plays. Rachel stands with her hands on her hips.
"See?" Ross says, pointing a finger at her.
"All right. You did what I said," Ross adds, his voice tight.
Rachel watches him silently.
"Yeah. And you know what? You're right. I do feel better. Thank you, Ross," Rachel says with a small nod, turning away.
Ross rubs his forehead with one hand, looking down.
The setting changes to a different apartment where Phoebe sits on a couch, a map spread on the table in front of her. She holds a phone to her ear.
"Yeah, in Albany," Phoebe says into the phone.
"Could I have the number of Frank Buffay?" she asks.
Phoebe holds a pen, looking down at the notepad.
"Okay. In Ithaca?" she asks into the phone.
Phoebe waits briefly.
"All right, Saratoga" Phoebe says, tracing the map with her pen.
Phoebe continues looking at the map.
"Oneonta," she says.
Phoebe raises her head slightly, her expression flat.
"You know what? You shouldn't call yourself information," Phoebe says into the phone.
Phoebe removes the phone from her ear.
In a living room decorated for Christmas with a lit tree, Phoebe sits on the floor by a coffee table covered with maps. Frances walks in through a door carrying a purple coat and a white bag.
Frances says, "Hey." Holding a pen over the maps, Phoebe looks up and replies, "Hello, Grandma. If that is, in fact, your real name."
Frances closes the door behind her.
Walking toward the center of the room, Frances tosses her coat onto a small table and says, "Come on now, Phoebs, don't still be mad at me."
Frances approaches a green couch.
Sitting on the edge of the couch, Frances asks, "How's it going?"
Phoebe lowers her gaze back to the coffee table.
Phoebe says, "Not so good."
Looking at her maps, Phoebe adds, "Upstate's pretty big."
Looking up at Frances, Phoebe says, "He's pretty small. You do the math." Audience laughter is audible.
Frances leans forward with her hands clasped. "Well, I think you're better off without him."
Frances reaches out and touches Phoebe's arm. "Oh, honey, I know he's your daddy, but well, to me, he's still"
Retracting her hand, Frances continues, "the irresponsible creep who knocked up your mom and stole her Gremlin." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe's shoulders slump slightly as she lowers her eyes to her hands, fidgeting with a map.
Looking up toward Frances, Phoebe says, "I just wanted to know who he was."
Frances remains seated, her hands clasped on her knees.
Frances nods and says, "Yeah, I know."
Frances begins to fidget with her fingers.
"Okay," Frances says.
Frances pauses, looking down briefly.
Meeting Phoebe's gaze, Frances says, "I wasn't completely honest with you when I told you that I didn't know exactly where he lived."
"What do you mean?" Phoebe asks, her eyebrows drawing together.
Gesturing slightly with her clasped hands, Frances replies, "He lives at 74 Laurel Drive in Middletown."
Frances raises her arm, extending her finger toward the hallway.
Still pointing, Frances says, "If you hit the Dairy Queen, you've gone too far. You take my cab," and holds out a set of keys.
Phoebe takes the keys. "Wow," she says.
Phoebe smiles, examining the keys in her hands.
"Thank you," Phoebe says.
Phoebe stands up, gathering her coat and mail. Frances also stands from the couch.
Pointing at Phoebe, Frances says, "Now, remember, nobody else drives that cab."
Holding her coat, Phoebe replies, "Got it."
Stepping toward Frances with a smile, Phoebe says, "I'm going to see my dad."
Phoebe opens her arms as she steps closer to Frances.
"Thank you," Phoebe says as they embrace.
The two women hug, patting each other's backs.
"Okay," Frances says as they step apart.
Phoebe turns and walks toward the hallway.
Pausing to look at a framed black-and-white photograph of an older man on a small table, Phoebe says, "Wish me luck, Grandpa."
Phoebe walks toward the exit as a door is heard opening. Frances turns to watch her, keeping her hands clasped. Audience laughter and cheering are audible.
Frances stands alone in the room, her gaze fixed toward the exit with a slight smile.
Frances's smile slowly fades to a neutral expression as the scene ends.
